nodes.title: Gojoseon Founded
nodes.description: Korea's first kingdom is founded by Dangun.

nodes.title: Fall of Gojoseon
nodes.description: Gojoseon falls following a year-long invasion by the Han Dynasty under Emperor Wu, leading to the creation of the Han Commanderies.

nodes.title: Silla Founded
nodes.description: The kingdom of Silla rises in the southeast.

nodes.title: Goguryeo Founded
nodes.description: Goguryeo emerges as a powerful northern kingdom.

nodes.title: Baekje Founded
nodes.description: Baekje is established in the southwest.

nodes.title: Gaya Confederacy
nodes.description: Gaya emerges as a confederacy of states in the Nakdong River basin.

nodes.title: Introduction of Buddhism
nodes.description: Buddhism is officially introduced to Goguryeo from Former Qin, later shaping culture and political systems.

nodes.title: Taehak (National Academy) Founded
nodes.description: King Sosurim of Goguryeo establishes Taehak, the first national academy in Korean history, to teach Confucianism.

nodes.title: Buddhism Introduced to Baekje
nodes.description: The Indian monk Marananta introduces Buddhism to Baekje during the reign of King Chimnyu.

nodes.title: King Gwanggaeto the Great
nodes.description: King Gwanggaeto the Great ascends the throne, launching massive military campaigns that expand Goguryeo's territory across Manchuria.

nodes.title: Silla Adopts Buddhism
nodes.description: Buddhism is officially adopted as the state religion in Silla following the martyrdom of Ichadon.

nodes.title: Battle of Gwansan Fortress
nodes.description: Silla forces defeat a joint Baekje-Gaya army, resulting in the death of Baekje's King Seong and Silla's control of the Han River valley.

nodes.title: Silla Expansion under King Jinheung
nodes.description: King Jinheung of Silla expands territory and erects monuments (Sunsubi) to mark the kingdom's new borders.

nodes.title: Hwarang Organization Formed
nodes.description: King Jinheung of Silla officially reorganizes the Hwarang, an elite youth group that played a key role in unifying the Three Kingdoms.

nodes.title: Battle of Salsu
nodes.description: Goguryeo forces under General Eulji Mundeok achieve a decisive victory against the invading Chinese Sui Dynasty at the Salsu River.

nodes.title: Cheomseongdae Built
nodes.description: Queen Seondeok of Silla commissions Cheomseongdae, the oldest surviving astronomical observatory in East Asia.

nodes.title: Battle of Ansi Fortress
nodes.description: Goguryeo forces successfully defend Ansi Fortress against a massive siege by the Tang Dynasty's invading army.

nodes.title: Unified Silla
nodes.description: Silla unifies most of the Korean peninsula.

nodes.title: National Academy (Gukhak) Established
nodes.description: King Sinmun establishes Gukhak, the national academy, to teach Confucian classics to elite students.

nodes.title: Balhae Founded
nodes.description: Dae Jo-yeong establishes Balhae, extending Korean history into Manchuria.

nodes.title: Hyecho's Travelogue Published
nodes.description: Silla monk Hyecho writes "Memoir of the Pilgrimage to the Five Kingdoms of India," describing his travels to India, Central Asia, and Persia.

nodes.title: Attack on Dengzhou
nodes.description: Balhae forces under King Mu launch a preemptive naval expedition against the Tang port of Dengzhou, asserting military strength.

nodes.title: Bulguksa & Seokguram
nodes.description: Construction begins on Bulguksa Temple and Seokguram Grotto in Gyeongju, representing the peak of Buddhist art and architecture.

nodes.title: Cheonghaejin Established
nodes.description: Jang Bogo establishes the Cheonghaejin maritime garrison on Wando Island, suppressing piracy and dominating East Asian trade.

nodes.title: Goryeo Dynasty
nodes.description: Goryeo reunites the peninsula and gives Korea its name.

nodes.title: Gwageo System Introduced
nodes.description: King Gwangjong introduces the national civil service examination system, reducing the power of local nobles and strengthening central rule.

nodes.title: Gukjagam Founded
nodes.description: King Seongjong establishes Gukjagam, the supreme national academy of Goryeo, to foster Confucian scholarship.

nodes.title: Battle of Gwiju
nodes.description: General Gang Gam-chan defeats the invading Liao (Khitan) forces at Gwiju, securing Goryeo's sovereignty.

nodes.title: Cheolli Jangseong Completed
nodes.description: Goryeo completes construction of the Cheolli Jangseong, a defensive wall spanning the northern border to defend against nomadic invasions.

nodes.title: Tripitaka Koreana
nodes.description: Goryeo carves over 80,000 wooden printing blocks of the Buddhist canon to appeal for divine protection against Mongol invaders.

nodes.title: Sambyeolcho Rebellion Begins
nodes.description: The elite Sambyeolcho military unit rebels against the Goryeo government's peace treaty with the Mongol Empire, initiating a multi-year resistance.

nodes.title: Jikji Published
nodes.description: The Jikji is printed using movable metal type at Heungdeoksa Temple, pre-dating Gutenberg's Bible by 78 years.

nodes.title: Joseon Dynasty
nodes.description: Yi Seong-gye founds the long-lived Joseon dynasty.

nodes.title: Hanyang Proclaimed Capital
nodes.description: The capital of Joseon is moved to Hanyang (modern-day Seoul) and construction begins on Gyeongbokgung Palace.

nodes.title: Veritable Records of the Joseon Dynasty
nodes.description: The compilation of the Joseon Wangjo Sillok begins, meticulously documenting the daily affairs of the Joseon kings for 472 years.

nodes.title: Rain Gauge (Cheugugi)
nodes.description: The world's first standardized rain gauge, Cheugugi, is invented during the reign of King Sejong the Great.

nodes.title: Hangul Created
nodes.description: King Sejong the Great creates the Korean alphabet, Hangul.

nodes.title: Gyeongguk Daejeon Completed
nodes.description: The Gyeongguk Daejeon, the code of law governing the Joseon Dynasty, is finalized and promulgated under King Seongjong.

nodes.title: Imjin War
nodes.description: Korea repels Japanese invasions under Admiral Yi.

nodes.title: Battle of Myeongnyang
nodes.description: Admiral Yi Sun-sin defeats a fleet of over 130 Japanese war vessels with only 13 ships under his command.

nodes.title: Dongui Bogam Published
nodes.description: Royal physician Heo Jun compiles the Dongui Bogam, a seminal encyclopedia of traditional Korean medicine.

nodes.title: Neutral Diplomacy of Gwanghaegun
nodes.description: King Gwanghaegun pursues a pragmatically neutral foreign policy to navigate tensions between the Ming Dynasty and Later Jin.

nodes.title: Manchu Invasion of Joseon
nodes.description: The Qing Dynasty invades Joseon, forcing King Injo to surrender at Namhansanseong Fortress and shift diplomatic ties to the Qing.

nodes.title: Late Joseon Renaissance
nodes.description: King Jeongjo drives a cultural and reform revival.

nodes.title: Suwon Hwaseong Fortress Completed
nodes.description: Construction of Suwon Hwaseong Fortress is completed under King Jeongjo, showcasing advanced military engineering and architecture.

nodes.title: Mokmin Simseo Published
nodes.description: Silhak scholar Jeong Yak-yong completes the "Mokmin Simseo," a seminal text on governance, corruption prevention, and social justice.

nodes.title: Daedongyeojido Created
nodes.description: Geographer Kim Jeong-ho publishes the Daedongyeojido, a highly detailed and accurate large-scale wooden map of Korea.

nodes.title: Gapsin Coup
nodes.description: Progressive reformists launch a coup to modernize Joseon's administration and eliminate class privilege, which is suppressed after three days.

nodes.title: Gwanghyewon Established
nodes.description: Royal physician Horace Newton Allen establishes Gwanghyewon, the first modern Western-style hospital in Korea.

nodes.title: Donghak Peasant Revolution
nodes.description: Peasants rise in rebellion demanding social reform and anti-foreign policies, triggering regional tensions.

nodes.title: Kabo Reforms
nodes.description: A series of sweeping modernizing reforms are introduced, abolishing the traditional class system, civil service exams, and slavery.

nodes.title: Korean Empire
nodes.description: Joseon is proclaimed the Korean Empire.

nodes.title: Japanese Occupation
nodes.description: Korea is annexed by Imperial Japan.

nodes.title: March 1st Movement
nodes.description: Nationwide peaceful protests demand independence, leading to the creation of the Provisional Government in Shanghai.

nodes.title: Battle of Qingshanli
nodes.description: Korean independence forces led by Kim Jwa-jin achieve a major military victory against Japanese troops in Manchuria.

nodes.title: Gwangju Student Movement
nodes.description: A large-scale student independence demonstration breaks out in Gwangju, spreading nationwide as a major anti-Japanese protest.

nodes.title: Korean Liberation Army
nodes.description: The military arm of the Provisional Government of the Republic of Korea is formed in Chongqing, coordinating with Allied forces.

nodes.title: National Liberation Day
nodes.description: Korea is liberated from Japanese rule following the surrender of Japan in World War II.

nodes.title: Republic of Korea
nodes.description: South Korea is established in the south.

nodes.title: North Korea (DPRK)
nodes.description: North Korea is established in the north.

nodes.title: Korean War
nodes.description: War breaks out following North Korea's invasion of the South.

nodes.title: Korean Armistice Agreement
nodes.description: An armistice agreement is signed, pausing the Korean War and establishing the Korean Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) near the 38th parallel.

nodes.title: April Revolution
nodes.description: Student-led protests overthrow the autocratic Syngman Rhee government.

nodes.title: May 16 Coup
nodes.description: Major General Park Chung-hee leads a military coup d'état, launching an era of military regime.

nodes.title: Miracle on the Han River
nodes.description: South Korea launches rapid export-led industrialization, growing from a war-torn country to a major global economy.

nodes.title: Saemaul Undong Movement
nodes.description: President Park Chung-hee launches Saemaul Undong (New Community Movement) to modernize the rural economy and improve living standards.

nodes.title: Gwangju Uprising
nodes.description: Pro-democracy demonstrations rise against military rule.

nodes.title: First Internet Connection
nodes.description: Korea becomes the second country in the world to establish an internet connection, linking Seoul National University and KIET.

nodes.title: June Democratic Struggle
nodes.description: Nationwide protests force the military regime to restore direct presidential elections, establishing the democratic Sixth Republic.

nodes.title: Seoul Olympics
nodes.description: South Korea hosts the Summer Olympics, marking its global entry.

nodes.title: UN Admission
nodes.description: Both South and North Korea simultaneously join the United Nations as full member states.

nodes.title: Seo Taiji and Boys Debut
nodes.description: The debut of Seo Taiji and Boys revolutionizes Korean popular music, marking the birth of modern K-pop.

nodes.title: Asian Financial Crisis
nodes.description: South Korea overcomes a severe economic crisis under an IMF program.

nodes.title: First Inter-Korean Summit
nodes.description: President Kim Dae-jung meets North Korean leader Kim Jong-il in Pyongyang, signing the June 15 Joint Declaration.

nodes.title: World Cup & Hallyu
nodes.description: Korea co-hosts the World Cup as the Korean Wave (Hallyu) spreads globally.

nodes.title: KTX High-Speed Rail Opens
nodes.description: South Korea launches the Korea Train Express (KTX), bringing the country into the era of high-speed rail travel.

nodes.title: Candlelight Revolution
nodes.description: Massive peaceful candlelight protests lead to the impeachment of President Park Geun-hye, demonstrating democratic maturity.

nodes.title: PyeongChang Winter Olympics
nodes.description: South Korea hosts the Winter Olympics in PyeongChang, notable for the joint march and unified teams of North and South Korea.

nodes.title: Global Peak of Hallyu
nodes.description: Korean pop culture reaches historic global heights, highlighted by film Parasite winning Best Picture at the Oscars and BTS topping the Billboard charts.
